codeReview.summary: The fix feature correctly verified VAL-CROSS-008 (Catalog Refresh Status Updates). No code changes were needed because the feature was already fully implemented by previous workers. The worker properly tested the complete flow: clicking refresh → amber dot + spinning icon during refresh → emerald dot + ready state after completion. Console was checked for errors (none found).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The status polling pattern in App.tsx uses different intervals based on state (3s for running, 15s for idle). This ensures responsive UI during refresh while reducing server load when idle.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: App.tsx:67-68 shows: const delay = data.state === "running" ? 3000 : 15000; timeoutId = window.setTimeout(poll, delay);

summary: Fix PASS. The fix feature adequately addressed VAL-CROSS-008 from the original cross-flow-interactions failure. The worker verified the complete catalog refresh status flow: (1) refresh button click triggers API call, (2) status indicator transitions to amber dot with spinning icon during running state, (3) progress counter updates (24/240 → 59/240 → etc.), (4) after completion (~90s), status returns to emerald dot + 'Ready' state. Code review confirms the implementation is correct: status polling with AbortController pattern, proper state transitions (idle/running/error), and dynamic UI indicators based on status.state. The original failure was due to missing verification, not missing implementation.
